title: Module 1
content:
Basics of Signals and Systems:
Definitions and Classification of Signals (Continuous-time and Discrete-
time, Periodic and Aperiodic, Even and Odd, Energy and Power signals) Basic
Operations on Signals (Scaling, Shifting, Folding)
Systems:
Definitions and Classification of Systems (Linear and Nonlinear, Time-
Invariant and Time-Variant, Causal and Non-Causal, Stable and Unstable)
Examples of Systems
System Properties:
Linear Time-Invariant (LTI) Systems ,Stability and Causality in LTI Systems
Representation of systems: Differential equation representation of
continuous time systems. Difference equation representation of discrete
systems.
Discrete time LTI systems and linear convolution.
Stability and causality of LTI systems.

title: Module 2
content:
Overview of Fourier Series:
Representation of Periodic Signals ,Convergence of Fourier series.
Continuous-Time Fourier Transform (CTFT)-Properties
Review of Laplace Transform, ROC of Transfer function, Properties of ROC,
Stability and causality conditions.
Relation between Fourier and Laplace transforms.

title: Module 3
content:
Discrete time fourier series for discrete periodic signals.
Properties of DTFS.
Discrete time fourier transform (DTFT) and its properties. Analysis
of discrete time LTI systems using DTFT.
Z transform, Mapping of s plane to Z plane ,ROC , Inverse transform,
properties,
Relation between DTFT and Z-Transform, Analysis of discrete time LTI
systems using Z transforms, Stability and causality using Z transform.

title: Module 4
content:
Sampling
Definition and importance of sampling in signal processing
Nyquist-Shannon Sampling Theorem, Conditions for perfect reconstruction,
Definition and causes of aliasing, Effects of undersampling, Methods to
prevent aliasing, Ideal vs. Practical Sampling, Ideal sampling (impulse
sampling), Practical sampling methods (zero-order hold, first-order hold)
Process of quantization, Quantization error and its effects, Uniform and non-
uniform quantization, Reconstruction of continuous-time signals from
discrete-time samples, Interpolation methods (zero-order hold, first-order
hold, sinc interpolation), Oversampling: benefits and applications, Under
sampling: conditions and applications
Sampling in the frequency domain, Sampling of band pass signals
Introduction   to  multi  rate  systems,  Decimation  and  interpolation.
Applications in digital signal processing
